page: Amazon Web Services r8ib.metal-48xl is a memory-optimized bare-metal server instance featuring Intel processors. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server delivers 192 virtual CPUs across 96 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing two threads per core. The dedicated CPU allocation operates at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz, delivering high-frequency compute capabilities alongside its substantial memory footprint.

The instance is equipped with 1536.0 GB of system memory, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per virtual core. This high memory-to-core ratio is designed to support memory-intensive workloads. The server does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached storage options.

For network connectivity, the r8ib.metal-48xl provides a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. It does not include any integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. As a bare-metal instance, it offers direct access to the underlying physical hardware, eliminating virtualization overhead and maximizing resource density for enterprise deployments.

This server is designed for large-scale databases, in-memory analytics, enterprise resource planning systems, and memory-bound scientific computing. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal architecture and high memory density provide a specialized resource profile that reduces virtualization overhead, making it suitable for workloads that require maximum memory capacity and high-speed networking without the need for local storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services r8ib.metal-48xl is a memory-optimized bare-metal server powered by Intel x86_64 processors. It features 192 dedicated virtual CPUs (96 physical cores) operating at 3.9 GHz, paired with 1536.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, relying on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. Designed for high resource density and zero virtualization overhead, this bare-metal server is optimized for memory-intensive enterprise applications. It is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and heavy data-processing workloads that demand significant memory capacity and high-speed network throughput.
